questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A thin uniform rod of length $2 \ell$, mass m is bent and pivoted at $A$ and connected to massless string as shown in figure whole system is in vertical plane. Now string is cut. Choose the correct options.
options: Maximum K.E. of system is $\frac{\mathrm{mg} \ell}{4}(1+\sqrt{10})$
Tension in string CD before cut is $\frac{3 \mathrm{mg}}{4}$
Moment of inertia about hing (A) is $m \ell^2$
Angle made by AB, $\theta$ with vertical is $\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{3}\right)$ where system has maximum K.E.
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{AO}=\sqrt{\left(\frac{3 l}{4}\right)^2+\left(\frac{l}{4}\right)^2}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\frac{\ell}{4} \sqrt{10} \\ & \mathrm{k} \cdot \mathrm{E}_{\mathrm{max}}=\frac{\mathrm{mg} \ell}{4}(1+\sqrt{10}) \\ & I_n=\left(\frac{\mathrm{m}}{2} \frac{\ell^2}{3}\right)+\left(\frac{\mathrm{m}}{2}\left(\frac{\ell^2}{12}\right)\right)+\frac{\mathrm{m}}{2}\left(\left(\frac{\ell \sqrt{5}}{2}\right)^2\right) \\ & =\frac{\mathrm{m} \ell^2}{2}\left(\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{12}+\frac{5}{4}\right) \\ & =\frac{\mathrm{m} \ell^2}{2}\left(\frac{4+1+3 \times 5}{12}\right)=\frac{5 \pi}{6} \mathrm{~m} \ell^2 \end{aligned}$ For tension before cutting Torque $\tau_n=0$ $\operatorname{mg}\left(\frac{3 l}{4}\right)=T(l) \Rightarrow T=\frac{3}{4} m g$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A disc is free to rotate about an axis passing through its centre and perpendicular to its plane. The moment of inertia of the disc about its rotation axis is I. A light ribbon is tightly wrapped over it in multiple layers. The end of the ribbon is pulled out at a constant speed of $u$. Let the radius of the ribboned disc be R at any time and thickness of the ribbon be $d(\lt \lt R)$.
options: Angular acceleration of the disc at any instant is proportional to $\frac{1}{R^3}$, where $R$ is radius of the ribboned disc.
Angular acceleration of the disc at any instant is proportional to $\frac{1}{R^2}$, where $R$ is the radius of the ribboned disc.
Force required to pull the ribbon at constant speed $u$ is $F=\frac{I u^2 d}{2 \pi R^4}$.
Force required to pull the ribbon at constant speed $u$ is $\mathrm{F}=\frac{Iu^2 \mathrm{~d}}{\pi R^4}$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \omega R=u=\text { constant } \\
& \omega \frac{d R}{d t}+R \frac{d \omega}{d t}=0 \\
& \therefore \frac{d \omega}{d t}=-\frac{\omega}{R} \frac{d R}{d t}
\end{aligned}$

In time dt radius decreases by dR
$\begin{aligned}
& -2 \pi R d R=u d d t \\
& \therefore \frac{d R}{d t}=-\frac{u d}{2 \pi R} \\
& \frac{d \omega}{d t}=\alpha=\frac{\omega}{R} \frac{u d}{2 \pi R} \\
& \alpha=\frac{u^2 d}{2 \pi R^3} \\
& \tau=\frac{I u^2 d}{2 \pi R^3} \\
& \therefore F=\frac{I u^2 d}{2 \pi R^4}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A rod is free to rotate in horizontal plane about one of its end hinged at point 'O' as shown in figure. Point P \& Q are fixed points located ir horizontal plane in such away that PO is perpendicular to rod \& QO is along length of rod. A bullet hits the rod perpendicularly at some distance from point ' $O$ ' and gets imbedded into it. Then :
options: Linear momentum of rod + bullet system will decrease after collision.
Linear momentum of rod + bullet system may increase
Angular momentum of rod + bullet system about point ' $P$ ' will not change before \& after collision.
Angular momentum of rod + bullet system about ' $Q$ ' may increase after collision.
questions.solution: Force applied by hinge on rod may be towards P , away from P or zero depending upon distance from point ' $O$ '. Accordingly linear momentum of rod + bullet system may decrease increase or remains constant respectively. Similarly angular momentum of system about ' $Q$ ' may decrease, increase or remain constant respectively.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform ball of radius R rolls without slipping between the rails such that the horizontal distance is $\sqrt{3} \mathrm{R}$ between the two contact points of the rails of the ball. Figure (a) shows the front view of the ball and figure (b) shows the side view of the ball. $\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{CM}}$ is the velocity of centre of mass of the ball and $\omega$ is the angular velocity of the ball after rolling down a distance 2h along the incline then
options: $\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{CM}}=\omega \mathrm{R}$
$\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{CM}}=\omega \frac{\mathrm{R}}{2}$
$\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{CM}}=\sqrt{\frac{10 \mathrm{gh}}{13}}$
$\mathrm{V}_{\mathrm{CM}}=\sqrt{\frac{10 \mathrm{gh}}{7}}$
questions.solution: $v_{C M}=\omega \sqrt{R^2-\frac{3 R^2}{4}}=\omega \frac{R}{2}$ (from pure rolling) $\mathrm{mgh}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{cm}}^2+\frac{1}{2} \frac{2}{5} \mathrm{mR}^2 \omega^2$ (from conservation of energy) $\therefore v_{C M}=\sqrt{\frac{10 \mathrm{gh}}{13}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two identical ring of equal mass ' $m$ ' and radius ' $R$ ' rigidly attached wears a bead of mass ' $m$ '. Bead can freely slide without friction. It is hinged at point ' $P$ ' in such a way that it can freely rotates about horizontal axis passing through point ' $P$ '. The ring and bead are released from rest from give position. Then, just after release
options: The magnitude of angular acceleration of ring is $\frac{5 g}{17 R}$
The magnitude of angular acceleration of rod is $\frac{2 g}{9 R}$
The magnitude of acceleration of bead is $\frac{2 g}{7}$
The magnitude of acceleration of bead is $\frac{\sqrt{85} g}{9}$
questions.solution:
For Bead,
$\begin{aligned} & 2 m g R-R_x R=8 m R^2 \alpha ~(1)\\ & \mathrm{R}_x=m a_x ~(2)\\ & \mathrm{a}_x=\alpha R ~(3) \end{aligned}$ From (1),(2) \& (3) $\begin{aligned} & \alpha=\frac{2 g}{9 R} \\ & m g=m a_y \Rightarrow a_y=g \end{aligned}$ acceleration of bead $=\frac{\sqrt{85} g}{9}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics

questions.q: A rod bent at right angle along its centre line, is placed on a rough horizontal fixed cylinder of radius R as shown in figure. Mass of rod is 2 m and rod is in equilibrium. Assume that friction force on rod at A and B are equal in magnitde.
options: Normal force applied by cylinder on rod at A is $3 \mathrm{mg} / 2$.
Normal force applied by cylinder on rod at B must be zero.
Friction force acting on rod at B is upward.
Normal force applied by cylinder on rod at A is mg .
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle of mass ' $m$ ' is attached to the rim of a uniform disc of mass ' $m$ ' and radius $R$. The disc is rolling without slipping on a stationary horizontal surface, as shown in the figure. At a particular instant, the particle is at the top most position and centre of the disc has speed $v_0$ and its angular speed is $\omega$. Choose the correct regarding the motion of the system (disc + particle) at that instant.
options: $v_0=\omega R$
kinetic energy of the system is $\frac{11}{4} m v_0^2$
speed of point mass $m$ is less than $2 v_0$
$\left|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}_{\mathrm{C}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}_{\mathrm{B}}\right|=\left|\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}_{\mathrm{B}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}_{\mathrm{D}}\right|$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: AB is a uniform rod of mass 1 kg and length 1 m . Rod is placed on a smooth horizontal table in xy plane along X -axis. Initially centre of mass C of the rod is at origin as shown. A constant force $\vec{F}=1 \hat{j}$ Newton starts acting on the rod at end A. Choose correct options:
options: C will move on Y -axis
after a finite time rod will be parallel to Y -axis momentarily.
rod undergoes oscillatory motion about point A after some time.
angular velocity of rod will be proportional to $\sqrt{\sin \theta}$ where $\theta$ is angle made by rod with X -axis, $(0 \leq \theta \lt \pi / 2)$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{C}}=\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{C}} \alpha \\ & \quad\left(\frac{1}{2} \cos \theta\right) 1=\frac{1(1)^2}{12} \frac{\mathrm{~d}^2 \theta}{\mathrm{dt}^2}\end{aligned}$
$\int_0^\omega \omega d \omega=6 \int_0^\omega \cos \theta \mathrm{d} \theta$
$\begin{aligned} & \frac{\omega^2}{2}=6 \sin \theta \\ & \omega \propto \sqrt{\sin \theta}\end{aligned}$
$\mathrm{T}_{\mathrm{C}}=\frac{1}{2} \cos \theta$ positive so $\theta$ increases as t increases. So rod will be parallel to $y$-axis in finite time . C.O.M. moves on $y$-axis
[if necessary use $\left.\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{d \theta}{\sqrt{\sin \theta}}=2.6\right]$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A light rigid rod $A B$ of length $3 \ell$ has a point mass $m$ at end $A$ and a point mass $2 m$ at end $B$ is kept on a smooth horizontal surface. Point C is the center of mass of the system. Initially the system is at rest. The mass 2 m is suddenly given a velocity $\mathrm{v}_0$ towards right. Take Z axis to be perpendicular to the plane of the paper.
options: The minimum moment of inertia (about Z -axis), $\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{zz}}$ of the system is $5 \mathrm{~m} \ell^2$.
The magnitude of tension in the rod in subsequent motion is $\frac{2 \mathrm{mv}_0^2}{9 \ell}$
The ratio of moment of inertia about Z -axis at points A and $\mathrm{B}, \frac{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{zz}}^{\mathrm{A}}}{\mathrm{I}_{\mathrm{zz}}^{\mathrm{B}}}=2$
Point C remains stationary during subsequent motion.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The disc of radius $r$ is confined to roll without slipping at $A$ and $B$. If the plates have the velocities shown, then
options: linear velocity $\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{o}}=\mathrm{v}$
angular velocity of disc is $\frac{3 v}{2 r}$
angular velocity of disc is $\frac{2 v}{r}$
None of these
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & v_A=\omega_0 R-v_0=v \\ & \omega_0 R-v_0=v \qquad ...\mathrm{(i)}\\ & v_B=\omega_0 R+v_0=3 v \quad ...\mathrm{(ii)} \end{aligned}$ from equation (i) \& (ii) $2 \omega_0 R=4 \mathrm{~V} \Rightarrow \omega_0 \mathrm{R}=2 \mathrm{v}$ $\omega_0=\frac{2 v}{R} \text { from (1) } v_0=v$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Three particles, each of mass $m$ are placed at the points $\left(\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1, \mathrm{z}_1\right),\left(\mathrm{x}_2, \mathrm{y}_2, \mathrm{z}_2\right)$ and $\left(\mathrm{x}_3, \mathrm{y}_3, \mathrm{z}_3\right)$ on the inner surface of a paraboloid of revolution obtained by rotating the parabola, $\mathrm{x}^2$ $=4 a y$ about the $y$-axis. Neglect the mass of the paraboloid. ( y -axis is along the vertical)
options: The moment of inertia of the system about the axis of the paraboloid is $I=4 m a\left(y_1+y_2+y_3\right)$.
If potential energy at O is taken to be zero, the potential energy of the system is $m g\left(y_1+y_2+y_3\right)$.
If the particle at $\left(\mathrm{x}_1, \mathrm{y}_1, \mathrm{z}_1\right)$ slides down the smooth surface, its speed at O is $\sqrt{2 \mathrm{gy}_1}$
If the paraboloid spins about OY with an angular speed $\omega$, the kinetic energy of the system will be $2 \mathrm{ma}\left(\mathrm{y}_1+\mathrm{y}_2+\mathrm{y}_3\right) \omega^2$.
questions.solution:
For any point on the surface of paraboloid, $\left(x^2+z^2\right)=4 a y$
(A) $\mathrm{I}=\sum_{\mathrm{i}=1}^3 \mathrm{~m}\left(\mathrm{x}_1^2+\mathrm{z}_1^2\right)$ (distance of $\mathrm{m}_{\mathrm{i}}$ from y -axis is $\left.\sqrt{\mathrm{x}_{\mathrm{i}}^2+\mathrm{z}_{\mathrm{i}}^2}\right)=4 \mathrm{ma}\left(\mathrm{y}_1+\mathrm{y}_2+\mathrm{y}_3\right)$.
(B) $m g\left(x_1+x_2+x_3\right)=m g\left(y_1+y_2+y_3\right)$
(C) $m g x_1=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{mv}_1^2 \Rightarrow \mathrm{v}_1=\sqrt{2 \mathrm{gy}_1}$
(D) Distance $y$-mass $m_p$ from y-axis, $r_i=\sqrt{x_i^2+z_i^2}=\sqrt{4 a_i}$
$\mathrm{KE}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m} \omega^2\left(\mathrm{r}_1^2+\mathrm{r}_2^2+\mathrm{r}_3^2\right)=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{~m} \omega^2 4 \mathrm{a}\left(\mathrm{y}_1+\mathrm{y}_2+\mathrm{y}_3\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform rod of length 4 m and mass $2 \sqrt{2} \mathrm{~kg}$ revolves with constant angular velocity $\omega$ about a vertical axis through a smooth joint A at one extremity of the rod so that it describes a cone of semi vertical angle $53^{\circ}$ as shown in the figure. Choose the correct statement(s). $\left(\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2\right)$
options: The value of angular velocity $\omega$ is $2.5 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}$
The angle between hinge reaction at point A and vertical axis AC is $45^{\circ}$
The magnitude of hinge reaction at point $A$ is 40 N
The angular momentum of the rod is constant.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: $A$ rod $A B$ of mass $M$ and length $L$ lies on a smooth horizontal table. An impulse J is applied to end A as shown in the figure immediately after imparting the impulse:
options: $\text { The radius of curvature of trajectory of } A \text { as seen from the ground is } \frac{8}{9} L \text {. }$
$\text { The radius of curvature of trajectory of B as seen from the ground is } \frac{2}{9} L \text {. }$
The instantaneous axis of rotation is at a distance of $L / 6$ from the mid point of the rod.
$\text { the mid point of the rod will move along a straight line. }$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{R}=\frac{\mathrm{v}^2}{\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{c}}} \\ & \mathrm{v}=\frac{\mathrm{J}}{\mathrm{M}} \\ & \omega=\frac{\frac{\mathrm{J}}{2}}{\frac{\mathrm{MI}^2}{12}}=\frac{6 \mathrm{~J}}{\mathrm{ML}} .\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} \Rightarrow v_A & =v+\frac{\omega L}{2}=\frac{J}{M}+\frac{3 J}{M}=\frac{4 J}{M} \\ a_{C_A} & =\frac{\omega^2 L}{2}=\frac{18 J^2}{M^2 L} \\ \Rightarrow R_A & =\frac{v_A^2}{a_{e A}}=16 \frac{J^2}{M^2} \cdot \frac{M^2 L}{18 J^2}=\frac{8}{9} \mathrm{~L} \\ v_B & =v-\frac{\omega L}{2}=\frac{2 J}{M} \\ \Rightarrow R_B & =\frac{4 J^2}{M^2} \cdot \frac{M^2 L}{18 J^2}=\frac{2}{9} L\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A cylindrical shell, a spherical shell and a solid sphere are allowed to roll on an inclined rough surface of co-efficient of friction $\mu$ and inclination $\theta$. The correct statements are
options: If cylindrical shell can roll on inclined plane, all other objects will also roll
If all the objects have different mass and allowed to roll from same height, then K.E. of all the object will be different at the bottom of inclined plane.
work done by the frictional force will be zero if object are rolling.
frictional force will be equal for all the objects having same mass
questions.solution:
If $I=\frac{\mathrm{mR}^2}{\mathrm{~K}}, \mu_{\text {min }}=\frac{\tan \theta}{1+\mathrm{K}}$
K is minimum for cylindrical shell $\Rightarrow \mu_{\text {min }}=\frac{\tan \theta}{1+2}=\frac{1}{2} \tan \theta$ is maximum
$\therefore$ If shell can roll then other will also roll.
Force of frictional is static hence work done by force will zero w.r.t. ground.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle of mass ' $m$ ' is attached to the rim of a uniform disc of mass ' $m$ ' and radius R. The disc is rolling without slipping on a stationary horizontal surface, as shown in the figure. At a particular instant, the particle is at the top most position and centre of the disc has speed $v_0$ and its angular speed is $\omega$. Choose the correct regarding the motion of the system (disc + particle) at that instant.
options: $V_0=\omega R$
kinetic energy of the system is $\frac{11}{4} \mathrm{mv}_0^2$
speed of point mass $m$ is less than $2 v_0$
$\left|\vec{v}_C-\vec{v}_B\right|=\left|\vec{v}_B-\vec{v}_D\right|$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two identical disc having radius $R$ are rotating in opposite sense are joined with light rod having frictionless groove A and B and placed on rough horizontal surface as shown in figure. Initially translation velocity of each disc is zero. Then :-
options: Final velocity of $B$ is $\frac{R \omega_0}{6}$
Rod's length will increase initially due to stress
Finally rod has tensile stress
Mechanical energy will decrease
questions.solution:
Initially
at any instant 't'
Finally pure rolling takes place; $v_C=\omega^{\prime} R$ Angular momentum conservation gives $\left(2 \omega_0\right) \frac{\mathrm{mR}^2}{2}-\frac{\mathrm{mR}^2}{2}\left(\omega_0\right)=2 \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{R}+\frac{2 \mathrm{mR}^2}{2} \omega^{\prime}$ $\begin{aligned} & \frac{m R^2}{2} \omega_0=2 \mathrm{mv}_{\mathrm{C}} \mathrm{R}+\mathrm{mRv}_{\mathrm{C}}\left(\text { Since }_{\left.\mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{C}}=\omega^{\prime} \mathrm{R}\right)}\right. \\ & \mathrm{v}_{\mathrm{C}}=\frac{\mathrm{R} \omega_0}{6}\end{aligned}$ Second figure shows that rod is under stress \& hence its length will increase. Finally when the discs roll purely, there is no friction \& hence no tensile stress Since friction is kinetic therefore mechanical energy will decrease.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A uniform rod AB of length $\ell$ and mass M hangs from point A at which it is freely hinged in a car moving with velocity $\mathrm{v}_0$. The rod can rotate in vertical plane about the axis at A . If the car suddenly stops,
options: The angular speed $\omega$ with which the rod starts rotating is $\frac{3 \mathrm{v}_0}{2 \ell}$
The minimum value of $\mathrm{v}_0$ so that the rod completes the rotation $\sqrt{\frac{8}{3} \mathrm{~g} \ell}$
Loss of energy during the process $\frac{1}{8} \mathrm{Mv}_0^2$
There is no loss of energy
questions.solution: Angular momentum is conserved about O $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{Mv} \frac{\ell}{2}=\frac{\mathrm{m} \ell^2}{3} \omega \\ & \omega=\frac{3 \mathrm{v}}{2 \ell}\end{aligned}$ if rod has to complete the circle, $\begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} \mathrm{I} \omega_{\min }^2=\mathrm{Mg} \ell \Rightarrow \omega_{\min }=\sqrt{\frac{2 \mathrm{Mg} \ell \times 3}{\mathrm{M} \ell^2}} \\ & \Delta \mathrm{KE}=\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{Mv}_0^2-\frac{1}{2} \cdot \frac{\mathrm{M} \ell^2}{3} \cdot \omega^2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A cylinder as shown in the figure is filled with oil of viscosity $\eta$. Within the cylinder, a thin disc of radius $R$ is rotating with a constant angular velocity $\omega$ about its symmetrical, vertial axis. The separation between the disc and the horizontal plane of the cylinder is $y$. Viscous forces act on the disc. Chose the correct option(s). Due to viscous forces on the disc
options: the value of total torque is $\tau=\frac{\pi \eta \omega R^4}{y}$
the value of power developed is $=\frac{\pi \eta \omega^2 R^4}{y}$
total value of torque is $\tau=\frac{\eta \omega^2 R^4}{2 \pi y}$
the value of power developed is $\tau=\frac{\eta \omega^2 R^4}{\pi y}$
questions.solution:
Consider an element of radius $r$ and thickness dr Viscous force acting on it is given by $d F=2\left(\eta \cdot 2 \pi r d r \frac{\omega r}{y}\right)[\because$ Viscous force acts from both top and bottom contact layer of the dise)
$\begin{aligned} & d \tau=\text { torque }=r \cdot d F=4 \pi \eta \frac{\omega}{y} r^3 d r \\ & \text { Total torque } \tau=\frac{4 \pi \eta \omega}{y} \int_0^R r^3 d r \\ & \tau=\frac{\pi \eta \omega R^4}{y} \text { Power developed }=\tau \omega=\frac{\pi \eta \omega^2 R^4}{y}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A mass $m$ of radius $r$ is rolling horizontally without any slip with a linear speed $v$. It then rolls up to a height given by $\frac{3}{4} \frac{v^2}{g}$
options: the body is identified to be a disc or a solid cylinder
the body is a solid sphere
MOI of the body about instantaneous axis of rotation is $\frac{3}{2} m r^2$
MOI of the body about instantaneous axis of rotation is $\frac{2}{5} m r^2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
